User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Global HRM Network platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

profilePhotoURL.description: URL of the user's profile photo.

accountStatus.description: The user's account status (active, suspended, or pending).

emailVerified.description: Indicates whether the user's email address has been verified.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

uid.description: The admin's unique ID from Firebase Auth.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the admin was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Profile entity.

summary.description: A brief summary of the profile.

yearsOfExperience.description: The number of years of experience.

industries.description: List of industries the user has worked in.

skills.description: List of skills the user possesses.

certifications.description: List of certifications the user has obtained.

achievements.description: List of achievements the user has accomplished.

employmentHistory.description: List of the user's employment history.

verificationStatus.description: The profile's verification status (unverified, verified, or rejected).

profileScore.description: The profile's score, calculated by an AI algorithm.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the profile was last updated.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.description: Represents an achievement of an HR professional.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Achievement entity.

year.description: The year the achievement was earned.

proofURL.description: URL of the proof of the achievement (optional).

verified.description: Indicates whether the achievement has been verified.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Represents a user's subscription to Global HRM Network.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

plan.description: The subscription plan (free, pro, or enterprise).

status.description: The subscription status (active, canceled, or expired).

paymentProvider.description: The payment provider used for the subscription (e.g., Stripe).

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminLog entity.

adminId.description: Reference to User representing the admin who performed the action. (Relationship: User 1:N AdminLog)

action.description: The action performed by the admin.

targetUserId.description: Reference to User representing the user targeted by the admin action. (Relationship: User 1:N AdminLog)

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the action was performed.

remarks.description: Additional remarks or notes about the action.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Application entity.

yearsOfExperience.description: The applicant's years of HR experience.

linkedinUrl.description: URL of the applicant's LinkedIn profile.

expertise.description: An array of the applicant's primary areas of HR expertise, ordered by priority.

interest.description: The applicant's primary interest in joining the network.

status.description: The application status (pending, approved, or rejected).

submittedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the application was submitted.

profileScore.description: An AI-generated score (0-100) representing the overall profile quality.

relevanceScore.description: An AI-generated score (0-100) on how relevant the profile is to the selected expertise.

profileScoreFeedback.description: The reasoning behind the profile score.

relevanceScoreFeedback.description: The reasoning behind the relevance score.

assessments.description: Contains detailed AI-driven assessments based on different HR models.

HRExpertise.title: HR Expertise
HRExpertise.description: Represents a single area of HR expertise.

id.description: Unique identifier for the expertise area.

name.description: The name of the expertise area (e.g., 'Talent Acquisition').

DimensionScore.title: Dimension Score
DimensionScore.description: Represents a single scored dimension within an assessment model.

dimension.description: The name of the assessment dimension.

definition.description: Stores user data. User ID is used as the document ID. Includes core user information (fullName, email, role, accountStatus, emailVerified).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores admin user data. Admin ID from Auth is used as the document ID.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of the admin user.

definition.description: Stores HR professional profiles, associated with a specific user.  The document ID is the profileId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores HR professional achievements, associated with a specific user. The achievementId is the document id.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: achievementId
params.description: The unique ID of the achievement.

definition.description: Stores user subscription data. SubscriptionId is the document ID.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique ID of the subscription.

definition.description: Stores admin activity logs. LogId is the document ID.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique ID of the admin log.

definition.description: Stores early access applications. ApplicationId is the document ID.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique ID of the application.

definition.description: Stores the master list of HR expertise areas. Admins can manage this collection.

params.name: expertiseId
params.description: The unique ID of the expertise area.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to facilitate a secure and scalable HR networking platform (Global HRM Network). It emphasizes authorization independence and clear access patterns to ensure robust security rules and easy debuggability. Key aspects of the design include:

*   **Authorization Independence:**  Critical user attributes, especially those relevant for authorization (e.g., user role) are directly accessible at the user document level, avoiding the need for `get()` calls to other collections. For collaborative data, the 'members' map pattern is recommended, enabling role-based access without hierarchical dependencies.
*   **Structural Segregation:** Data with different security requirements is stored in separate collections. User data is in `/users`, while admin data is in a separate `/admins` collection. User-specific profiles are stored under `/users/{userId}/profile`, while admin logs are stored separately.
*   **Access Modeling:** The structure follows consistent patterns. User-owned data (profiles, achievements, subscriptions) is organized under the `/users/{userId}` path. Admin users are stored in their own top-level collection.
*   **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**  The design enables secure `list` operations by structuring data to allow rules to efficiently determine access based on path and user roles.  Public profile listing can be secured by a visibility status on the HR Profiles and Firestore rules that filters depending on that status.
*   **Invariants:** Ownership and timestamps are explicitly managed within the document structure to ensure data integrity. The `userId` field in subcollections explicitly denotes ownership.
*   **Managed Data:** A new `/hr_expertise` collection is added to store a configurable list of HR skills, allowing admins to maintain a consistent taxonomy for user profiles.

This structure enables simple and robust security rules, as well as supports the application's core features, including user authentication, role-based access control, HR profile management, profile verification workflow, subscription management, profile score calculation, and admin panel functionality.
